tasks.title: WAVE07 Testing: Clarify convenience versus source-of-truth verification at the owning packages
tasks.prompt: Use `lib/design/design-plan.json`, `../socialpredict/README/PRODUCTION-NOTES/BACKEND/07-testing-strategy.md`, and `../socialpredict/README/PRODUCTION-NOTES/BACKEND/FUTURE/03-long-term-test-infrastructure.md` as the binding context for WAVE07 verification hardening. Start by tightening the existing package-local runtime, migration, and accounting-sensitive test files so they explicitly distinguish SQLite convenience coverage from behaviors that still require real-Postgres proof. Keep the changes close to the owning packages, refresh the active testing note only as needed to reflect the concrete source-of-truth targets chosen for this wave, and do not create a new shared test framework, top-level `testing/` tree, or CI program.
acceptance_criteria: The touched runtime, migration, or accounting-sensitive test files make it clear which checks are convenience-only and which behaviors are still awaiting real-Postgres verification.
The first real-Postgres targets for this wave are narrowed to concrete W04 and W06 risks rather than a broad integration backlog.
Any touched testing note changes stay consistent with package-local test ownership and keep long-term infrastructure ideas explicitly deferred.

focus: continued deferral of new testing-platform structure

out_of_scope: introducing a top-level `testing/` subsystem or shared `TestSuite` base
broad CI orchestration or coverage-gating redesign
testcontainers-first rollout or generalized integration harness work
notes: Prefer comments, naming, and helper extraction inside the owning packages over a central test taxonomy.
Use the smallest documentation edit that makes the chosen truth boundary explicit.

tasks.title: WAVE07 Testing: Add Postgres-truthful runtime and migration verification for readiness and startup-writer behavior
tasks.prompt: With the target seams narrowed, add the first real-Postgres verification where SQLite is not credible: runtime DB readiness after startup, migration failure posture, and the chosen startup-writer or migration-serialization behavior from W04. Keep the tests beside `internal/app/runtime` and `migration` or in the smallest justified backend-root contract location, reuse existing helpers where practical, and avoid broad container-orchestration or CI redesign. If minimal production seams must change to make the behavior testable, keep them owned by runtime or migration code rather than inventing a new test platform.
acceptance_criteria: Real-Postgres-backed checks exist for the touched readiness, startup-writer, or migration behaviors whose correctness depends on Postgres semantics.
The new DB-truthful checks live with the owning runtime or migration code, or in a narrowly justified backend-root contract test, without introducing a central test subsystem.
The touched verification makes failure posture explicit for DB loss, schema incompatibility, or startup-writer discipline without widening into general deployment orchestration.

focus: real-Postgres verification for readiness and startup mutation behavior
migration failure posture under the owned startup seam
package-local or narrowly justified contract-test placement

out_of_scope: caching, Redis, or unrelated database architecture changes
notes: Prefer the smallest real-Postgres cases that prove the runtime contract; do not duplicate every SQLite case against Postgres.
If startup-writer behavior cannot be proven entirely package-local, use backend-root or `server` only for the narrow cross-boundary seam.

tasks.title: WAVE07 Testing: Add Postgres-truthful verification for the first atomic accounting workflow
tasks.prompt: Add the next small real-Postgres verification slice to the first accounting-sensitive workflow that W04 made atomic, expected to be bet placement unless the completed W04 slice leaves a smaller live unit of work. Prove transaction boundaries, rollback behavior, and the chosen primary-DB concurrency protection under overlapping requests or equivalent direct repository or service calls. Keep the tests in the owning domain or repository packages, preserve integer-first accounting assumptions, and do not grow a generalized transaction-test framework.
acceptance_criteria: The first migrated atomic workflow has real-Postgres verification for transaction success, rollback, and the touched concurrency-control behavior.
The verification stays in the owning domain or repository packages and does not depend on a new shared transaction-test platform.
The tests preserve accounting semantics and avoid speculative coverage of workflows that the active queue has not yet migrated.

focus: transaction and rollback truth under Postgres
concurrency protection for the first atomic workflow

out_of_scope: sell-position or market-resolution sweeps beyond the first migrated workflow
notes: Start with the workflow W04 actually migrated instead of inventing a new accounting surface.
Add only the smallest concurrency cases needed to prove the chosen locking or update semantics.

tasks.title: WAVE07 Testing: Harden boundary contract tests for auth, rate limiting, and shared failure vocabulary
tasks.prompt: Strengthen the request-boundary verification that earlier waves introduced by extending existing auth, security, server, and OpenAPI contract tests around must-change-password exceptions, shared `405`, `429`, or sanitized `500` behavior, proxy-sensitive client-IP assumptions, and any touched public `reason` vocabulary. Keep the tests package-local or backend-root where they already live, and update `openapi.yaml` only if the hardened contract checks expose real drift. Do not reopen route redesign, distributed rate limiting, or generated-client work.
acceptance_criteria: Existing auth, security, server, or OpenAPI contract tests cover the touched shared-failure behaviors and documented auth exceptions more explicitly than before.
Any touched public `reason` vocabulary or route-spec expectations stay aligned between implementation and `openapi.yaml`.
The verification remains attached to the owning packages and backend-root contract tests rather than moving into a new shared test layer.

focus: shared failure vocabulary and auth-exception contract coverage
route-spec parity for any touched public failure shapes

out_of_scope: broader API versioning or generated-client changes
reopening unrelated handler migrations just to chase test coverage
notes: Prefer extending existing contract tests over adding duplicate suites.
Keep proxy-trust and shared-failure expectations explicit in test names and fixtures.

tasks.title: WAVE07 Testing: Stop and review remaining DB-truth gaps and deferred test-platform ideas
tasks.prompt: Finish WAVE07 with a verification-specific stop-and-review point. Reconcile what this wave actually proved against the remaining SQLite-only convenience coverage, make the next real-Postgres candidate explicit if one still remains, and keep `FUTURE/03-long-term-test-infrastructure.md` clearly deferred. Do not pull `testcontainers-go`, a top-level `testing/` tree, shared `TestSuite` architecture, broad fast/slow taxonomy work, performance suites, or CI-platform redesign into the active queue unless the touched evidence now reveals a specific uncovered behavior that cannot be handled with the current package-local posture.
acceptance_criteria: The remaining DB or runtime behaviors that still lack source-of-truth Postgres verification are explicitly inventoried after the wave's concrete test additions.
The active and future testing notes accurately distinguish completed hardening from still-deferred infrastructure ideas.
The wave ends with a precise next verification seam rather than a revived platform backlog.

focus: accuracy of active versus deferred testing notes
continued rejection of speculative testing-platform expansion

out_of_scope: broad CI or workflow taxonomy redesign
centralized shared test framework or helper-repo extraction
notes: This is the WAVE07 stop-and-review point before any heavier verification or CI discussion.
Keep the next slice framed as one concrete uncovered behavior, not a testing-platform program.
